answer:
1. Disease Name
revention
The leaf signs match: Leaves curl upward, leaves become thick and small, plant remains short. Likely reason: Virus spread by whiteflies.

2. What You Should Do Now
- Start now: Remove highly infected plants, control whiteflies, use sticky traps.
- Remove badly affected leaves.
- Keep leaves dry after watering.
- Check the plant again after 2-3 days.
- If spreading fast, ask local agri officer.

3. Precautions
- Healthy seedlings, insect-proof nursery, weed control around field.
- Do not overwater the plant.
- Keep space between plants for airflow.
- Check leaves every day for new spots or yellowing.
- Clean tools after use.

1. Disease Name
on
The leaf signs match: Brown leaf margins, weak stems, poor fruit quality. Likely reason: Low potassium availability.

2. What You Should Do Now
- Start now: Add potash fertilizer, irrigate after application.
- Remove badly affected leaves.
- Keep leaves dry after watering.
- Check the plant again after 2-3 days.
- If spreading fast, ask local agri officer.

3. Precautions
- Soil testing, balanced NPK application, add crop residue compost.
- Do not overwater the plant.
- Keep space between plants for airflow.
- Check leaves every day for new spots or yellowing.
- Clean tools after use.
I do not have enough information in the knowledge base.

1. Disease Name
Prevention
The leaf signs match: Black circular sunken spots on peel, fruit flesh may soften under spots. Likely reason: Fungal infection.

2. What You Should Do Now
- Start now: Remove affected fruits, keep fruits dry, use recommended fungicide program in field.
- Remove badly affected leaves.
- Keep leaves dry after watering.
- Check the plant again after 2-3 days.
- If spreading fast, ask local agri officer.

3. Precautions
- Sanitize crates, avoid rough handling, store in cool dry place.
- Do not overwater the plant.
- Keep space between plants for airflow.
- Check leaves every day for new spots or yellowing.
- Clean tools after use.

1. Disease Name
Prevention
The observed signs match: Black circular sunken spots on peel, fruit flesh may soften under spots. Likely reason: Fungal infection.

2. What You Should Do Now
- Start now: Remove affected fruits, keep fruits dry, use recommended fungicide program in field.
- Remove badly affected plant parts.
- Keep harvested fruits dry and handle gently.
- Check more fruits for similar internal or surface damage.
- If spreading fast, ask local agri officer.

3. Precautions
- Sanitize crates, avoid rough handling, store in cool dry place.
- Do not overwater the plant.
- Keep space between plants for airflow.
- Check the affected part every day for new damage.
- Clean tools after use.
